zh-tw.title: 狗狗語錄
zh-tw.description: 從毛孩的角度看人類，從狗主人的角度看自己——精選最有畫面的狗狗語錄、柴犬厭世金句與養狗才懂的日常哲學

en.title: Dog Quotes
en.description: From the dog's-eye view of human life to the truths only dog owners know — heartwarming, hilarious, and very much earned

items.sourceNote: 毛孩看到主人回家的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是出門倒個垃圾，三分鐘後回來。

但對牠來說，那是整整一個世紀。

所以牠才會用全身的力氣搖尾巴，
好像在說：你終於回來了，地球差點就要停止運轉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有狗的朋友——他下班開門那一刻，就會想起這段話
variations: 更新版：就算你只是去廁所兩分鐘出來，牠還是會用同樣熱情的方式迎接你。在牠的時間單位裡，沒有「短暫」這件事。

en.content:
You stepped outside to take out the trash. You came back three minutes later.

But for your dog, that was an entire century.

That's why its whole body wags when it sees you again,
as if to say: you finally came home, the planet almost stopped spinning.
en.editorNote: Send to any dog owner — they'll picture the exact moment they walk through their door
variations: Bonus: even if you just went to the bathroom for two minutes, you get the same homecoming. In dog time, the word 'brief' does not exist.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗散步耍賴行為，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
「來，我們回家了。」

狗狗：（直接四腳朝地，平躺路中央）

你拉牽繩，牠多加 30 公斤的隱形重量。
你蹲下來哄，牠閉眼裝睡。
你假裝要走，牠睜一隻眼確認你會不會真的拋棄牠。

這不是散步，這是談判。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養柴犬、米克斯、或任何「個性很有意見」的狗主人——他立刻就有畫面
variations: 進階版：你假裝走遠了，牠終於起身——但走兩步又躺下，告訴你：我願意走，但要照我的節奏。

en.content:
"Okay, time to go home."

Dog: (instantly flops onto the sidewalk, all four legs out)

You tug the leash — it adds 30 invisible kilograms.
You kneel down to sweet-talk it — it closes its eyes and pretends to nap.
You fake walking away — it opens one eye to confirm whether you'll actually abandon it.

This is not a walk. This is a negotiation.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner of a Shiba, mixed-breed, or any dog with strong opinions — they have lived this exact scene
variations: Advanced version: you walk far enough that the dog finally gets up — only to take two steps and lie down again, just to clarify that the walk continues on its terms, not yours.

items.sourceNote: 毛主人對狗的對話模式，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，你會開始用三歲小孩的語氣，
對著一個聽不懂中文的生物說：

「寶貝你最棒了～」
「這是誰呀～是不是壞壞～」
「不可以唷～這個不可以吃～」

然後在外面，你又是一個正常的大人。
但只有你自己知道，那個三歲小孩的語氣，
才是你真正的本體。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給任何一個「在狗面前完全變成另一個人」的朋友——他會邊笑邊承認
variations: 附帶觀察：這個語氣，會無縫切換到對家人的小孩、對伴侶、最後對自己。狗狗，是你內在那個小孩的解封鑰匙。

en.content:
After you get a dog, you start using a baby voice
to talk to a creature that doesn't understand any human language:

"Who's a good boy~ you are~"
"Is that your toy? Is that your toy?!"
"No no no, we don't eat that, we don't eat that."

And then in public, you're a normal adult again.
But you and only you know — the baby voice is the real you.
The adult is just the cover story.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who turns into a completely different person around their dog — they'll laugh and admit it
variations: Bonus observation: that voice will eventually leak out toward babies, partners, and finally yourself. The dog is the key that unlocks your inner soft creature.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬厭世表情經典場景，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
柴犬的表情有三種：

第一種：你誰？
第二種：又是你？
第三種：你還沒走？

第四種其實也有，但只在你拿出零食的瞬間出現。
那個瞬間，牠會告訴你：好啦，我們是朋友。
（然後吃完繼續第二種。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有柴犬的朋友——他每天都在被自己的狗用眼神社交懲罰
variations: 進階版：當你想擁抱柴犬，牠會微微側身、用一隻後腿輕輕推開你。這不是拒絕，這是一份明確劃定的距離說明書。

en.content:
A Shiba Inu has three default facial expressions:

One: who are you?
Two: oh, you again?
Three: you're still here?

There's a fourth, but it only appears the second you open a bag of treats.
In that moment, it briefly admits: fine, we're friends.
(Then it finishes chewing and resets to expression number two.)
en.editorNote: Send to any Shiba owner — they get socially humbled by their own dog daily
variations: Advanced version: when you try to hug a Shiba, it tilts its body just slightly and pushes you away with one back leg. This is not rejection — it is a clearly written boundary statement.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗乞食眼神觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是想安靜吃個便當。

但你身邊那雙眼睛說：

「我從出生到現在從來沒有吃過任何東西。」
「你手上那塊雞肉，會改變我的一生。」
「如果你不分我，我會記得，我會永遠記得。」

你最後當然分了。
因為那不是眼神，那是國家級的情緒勒索。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天在餐桌邊被狗狗用眼神勒索的朋友——他知道自己已經輸太多次了
variations: 附帶警告：你只要分過一次，那個眼神會升級成「定期繳費系統」，每餐都會準時出現在你旁邊。

en.content:
You just wanted to eat a quiet meal.

But the eyes next to you are saying:

"I have never eaten anything in my entire life."
"That piece of chicken in your hand will change me as a person."
"If you don't share, I will remember. I will remember forever."

You shared, of course.
Because those aren't eyes. That's a fully credentialed emotional negotiator.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who loses every staring contest at dinner — they already know they've surrendered too many times
variations: Warning: share food once, and those eyes upgrade into a recurring subscription. They will appear next to your plate at every single meal from then on.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗忠誠陪伴主題，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠不知道你今天工作多累。
牠不知道你被誰誤會、被誰冷落。
牠不知道你為什麼坐在沙發上不說話。

牠只知道：你回來了，你在這裡，那一切就還可以。

所以牠會走過來，把頭靠在你腳邊。
那不是寵物，那是這個世界送你的小型穩定器。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近過得有點累、家裡有狗的朋友——他會看完這段，然後低頭看一下他的狗
variations: 延伸版：你流眼淚的時候，牠不會問你怎麼了，牠只會更靠近一點。原來最高級的安慰，是不需要語言的。

en.content:
Your dog doesn't know how exhausting your day was.
It doesn't know who misunderstood you or who let you down.
It doesn't know why you're sitting on the couch saying nothing.

It only knows: you're home, you're here, so things are okay again.

That's why it walks over and rests its head against your foot.
That's not a pet. That's the small stabilizer the world sent you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been a little tired lately and has a dog at home — they'll finish reading and look down at theirs
variations: Extended version: when you cry, the dog doesn't ask what happened. It just moves closer. Turns out the highest form of comfort doesn't need language.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡前的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你還沒拿出洗澡盆。
你還沒打開水龍頭。
你只是「想到」要幫狗狗洗澡。

牠就已經消失了。

剛剛還在客廳搖尾巴的生物，現在縮在沙發底下，眼神帶著「你怎麼可以」的譴責。

狗狗沒有讀心術，牠只是把你研究了五年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次洗澡都要把狗從沙發底下拖出來的朋友——他會邊苦笑邊點頭
variations: 進階版：你假裝沒事繼續滑手機，等牠放鬆走出來，再突然抓起牠衝向浴室。這個策略，每次都失敗，但每次你都還是會試。

en.content:
You haven't taken out the bathtub yet.
You haven't turned on the faucet yet.
You just thought about giving the dog a bath.

It's already gone.

The creature that was wagging its tail in the living room is now jammed under the sofa, looking at you with full "how dare you" energy.

Dogs aren't telepathic. They've just studied you for five years.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has to extract a dog from under the couch on bath day — they'll wince and nod
variations: Advanced version: you pretend nothing's happening and scroll your phone. The moment the dog relaxes and walks out, you grab it and sprint to the bathroom. This plan fails every time. You will try it again next week.

items.sourceNote: 狗主人手機相簿觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，你的手機相簿會出現一個很奇怪的現象：

7000 張狗狗的照片。
400 張狗狗的影片。
3 張你自己的照片，而且其中兩張，狗狗也在裡面。

你已經不是這個帳號的主角了。
你是一個攝影師，全職拍一個不會看你成品的客戶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次打開相簿都覺得「我自己呢？」的毛主人——他會立刻去檢查自己的相簿
variations: 附帶觀察：你發 IG 限動，狗狗的那則永遠比你出去玩的那則收到更多愛心。你接受了這個事實，因為你也覺得狗狗比較可愛。

en.content:
After you get a dog, a strange thing happens to your camera roll:

7,000 photos of the dog.
400 videos of the dog.
3 photos of yourself, two of which also feature the dog.

You are no longer the main character of this account.
You're a full-time photographer for a client who will never review your work.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose camera roll has been quietly taken over — they'll check it immediately and laugh
variations: Bonus: your dog posts on Instagram always get more likes than your vacation photos. You accepted this peacefully, because deep down, you also think the dog is cuter.

items.sourceNote: 雨天狗狗散步抗議，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
下雨天，你穿好雨衣、套好牠的小雨衣，準備帶牠出門。

牠走到門口，看了一下外面。
牠抬頭看你。
牠回頭看一下沙發。
牠又看了一下你，眼神告訴你：「我們今天可以不去嗎？」

你考慮了 2 秒。
你脫下雨衣。
你跟牠一起回沙發。

誰是主人，這時候已經很清楚了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次雨天都會被狗狗說服改成「在家陪坐」的主人——這就是真實生活
variations: 進階版：晴天的時候，牠會在門口暴衝。牠的態度，是天氣決定的，不是你決定的。

en.content:
It's raining. You put on your raincoat, fasten the dog's tiny matching one, and head for the door.

It walks to the entrance. Looks outside. Looks up at you.
Looks back at the couch.
Looks at you again, with eyes that say: "Could we just… not, today?"

You think about it for 2 seconds.
You take off the raincoat.
You walk back to the couch with the dog.

Who the owner is here — that's been settled for a while now.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has been quietly persuaded by their dog to stay in on rainy days — this is everyday life now
variations: Advanced version: on sunny days, the same dog turbo-launches at the door. Its attitude is determined by the weather, not by you.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗門口迎接的工作觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你家狗狗的職業是：全職門口接待員。

工作內容：
上班時間 24 小時。
沒有休息日。
薪水是兩餐飼料、一條牽繩、無限量的擁抱。

牠每天最重要的 KPI，就是讓你回家的那一刻，覺得自己是世界上最被期待的人。

而牠每天都達標。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養狗的朋友——這是他每天回家最被需要的「公司福利」
variations: 附加條款：偶爾請假的時候，牠會用沙發鞋子作為一份不滿意度評語，提醒你他的工作不該被忽視。

en.content:
Your dog has a full-time job: door greeter.

Responsibilities:
On shift 24 hours a day.
No days off.
Compensation: two meals of kibble, one leash, unlimited hugs.

Its most important daily KPI is to make sure that the moment you walk through the door, you feel like the most anticipated person in the world.

It hits the target every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to any dog owner — this is the most reliable employee benefit in their life
variations: Fine print: when the greeter takes a personal day, you'll find a chewed shoe on the couch — that's a written performance review reminding you that the job exists.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬抗拒散步躺地經典場景，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
柴犬散步的步驟：

1. 你說「走吧」，牠興奮衝出門。
2. 走 50 公尺，牠突然停下。
3. 牠決定現在這裡是世界中心。
4. 牠平躺。
5. 你拉牽繩，牠變成 30 公斤的石頭。
6. 路人經過，露出同情的微笑。
7. 你也微笑回應，假裝這一切都在計畫中。

計畫從來不在你手上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個被柴犬「定點抗議」過的主人——他知道這個畫面太真實
variations: 結局版：等牠決定起身，你已經感謝了三位路人、解釋了五次「牠就是這樣」，並且重新評估了你跟一隻狗的權力關係。

en.content:
How a Shiba Inu walk works:

1. You say "let's go," the dog explodes out the door.
2. 50 meters in, it stops cold.
3. It decides this exact spot is now the center of the universe.
4. It lies down.
5. You pull the leash. It becomes a 30-kilogram boulder.
6. A stranger walks by, smiling sympathetically.
7. You smile back, as if all of this was the plan.

The plan was never yours.
en.editorNote: Send to any Shiba owner who has been forced into a public sit-down protest — this is uncomfortably accurate
variations: Ending: by the time the dog decides to stand up, you've thanked three strangers, explained "it just does this" five times, and quietly reassessed your entire power dynamic with a small fluffy animal.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗等門等待主人回家觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你出門那一刻，牠就坐在門口。

牠不知道你去哪裡。
牠不知道你會去多久。
牠只知道：這扇門，是你會回來的地方。

所以牠就坐在那裡。
從早到晚。
從晴天到下雨。
從你還愛自己，到你已經不太喜歡自己。

牠都還在等。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近覺得自己不夠好、家裡有狗的朋友——這段話會讓他想起，原來有人從來沒有放棄相信他
variations: 附加版：你終於開門那一刻，牠不會問你去哪、為什麼這麼晚——牠只會用全部的力氣告訴你：你回來了，這就夠了。

en.content:
The moment you leave, your dog sits at the door.

It doesn't know where you went.
It doesn't know how long you'll be gone.
It only knows: this door is where you come back.

So it sits there.
From morning to night.
From sunshine to rain.
From the version of you who still liked yourself, to the version that's been struggling lately.

It's still waiting.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been hard on themselves lately and has a dog at home — they'll remember that someone never stopped believing in them
variations: When you finally open the door, it doesn't ask where you went or why you're late. It just tells you, with everything it has: you're home. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看到其他狗的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你家狗狗在路上看到另一隻狗。

選項一：原地暴衝、瘋狂搖尾巴，好像對方是失散三十年的親人。
選項二：完全裝沒看到，傲嬌地把頭轉開。
選項三：對方明明很小，牠卻嚇到躲到你身後，並且表示「我從來沒看過這種怪物」。

沒有人知道牠會選哪一個。
包括牠自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養狗、每次散步都在猜「今天會是哪個版本」的朋友——他每天都在驚喜
variations: 附帶觀察：對方主人會根據你家狗狗的反應，決定是要走過來打招呼，還是抱著自己的狗繞道走。這是一場無聲的社交談判。

en.content:
Your dog spots another dog on the street.

Option A: charges forward, tail spinning, as if reunited with a long-lost relative.
Option B: pretends to see nothing, turns its head away with full aristocratic energy.
Option C: the other dog is tiny — but yours hides behind your leg and signals "I have never seen such a creature in my life."

No one knows which one will play.
Including the dog.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who plays the daily lottery of "which version of my dog will show up today" — every walk is a surprise
variations: Bonus observation: the other owner adjusts their plan based on your dog's reaction. They either walk over to say hi, or scoop up their dog and reroute. It's a silent social negotiation, and your dog is leading it.

items.sourceNote: 毛孩陪伴度過低潮主題，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
人會離開。工作會結束。朋友會走散。
但你回家時，牠永遠會在門口。

而且牠不會問：你最近還好嗎？
牠會直接告訴你：你還好，因為我在這裡。

養狗最神奇的事情，不是牠陪了你多少天，
而是那些你以為自己撐不過去的日子，
你都撐過去了——而牠就睡在你旁邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近過得很辛苦、家裡有毛孩的朋友——這段話會落在他需要被接住的位置
variations: 延伸版：你以為是你在養牠。其實很多時候，是牠在養你。一個眼神、一次靠近，就讓你願意再多撐一天。

en.content:
People leave. Jobs end. Friendships drift apart.
But when you come home, your dog is always at the door.

And it doesn't ask: how have you been?
It just tells you: you're okay, because I'm here.

The magical thing about having a dog isn't how many days it's been with you.
It's that all those days you thought you couldn't get through —
you got through.
And it was sleeping next to you the whole time.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been having a tough stretch and has a dog at home — this will land exactly where they need it to
variations: Extended: you thought you were the one taking care of the dog. Most of the time, the dog has been quietly taking care of you. One look, one nudge closer — and you decide to make it through one more day.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗睡覺佔床觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你買了一張很貴的雙人床。

第一天晚上，你睡正中央。
第二天晚上，狗狗來了，你睡左邊三分之二。
第三天晚上，你睡邊邊一條，棉被被牠捲走一半。
第四天晚上，你掛在床沿，慶幸自己還沒掉下去。

那張床的所有權，從第二天就已經不是你的了。
你只是繳貸款的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天晚上跟狗狗搶床、最後總是輸掉的朋友——他會邊苦笑邊認領這個身份
variations: 進階版：你想翻身，狗狗會發出一聲低沉的嘆氣，提醒你：你的動作打擾到了真正的房客。

en.content:
You bought an expensive queen-size bed.

Night one: you sleep in the middle.
Night two: the dog joins, you sleep on the left third.
Night three: you're on a strip near the edge, half the blanket has been claimed.
Night four: you're hanging off the side, grateful you haven't fallen.

The ownership of that bed transferred on night two.
You are just the person paying the mortgage on it.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who quietly loses the bed war every night — they'll laugh and accept the title
variations: Advanced version: you try to roll over and the dog lets out a deep sigh, reminding you that your movement just inconvenienced the real tenant of the bed.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對快遞員與門鈴的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
門鈴一響，你家狗狗的反應分三段：

第一段：地震級狂吠，好像家裡來了八個入侵者。
第二段：你開門，是熟悉的快遞大哥。
第三段：牠尾巴搖得比你還開心，繞著對方腳邊轉圈，順便幫你簽收。

你問牠剛剛兇什麼。
牠看你一眼，意思是：「我只是先建立氣勢，這是禮節。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次門鈴一響就要先安撫狗狗的朋友——這個流程他每週都跑好幾遍
variations: 附加觀察：等快遞大哥走了，牠還會多吠兩聲，告訴你：「我剛剛守住了這個家，記得加菜。」

en.content:
When the doorbell rings, your dog's response comes in three acts:

Act one: earthquake-level barking, as if eight intruders just broke in.
Act two: you open the door — it's the same delivery guy as always.
Act three: tail wagging at full speed, circling his legs, basically signing for the package on your behalf.

You ask what all the barking was about.
The dog gives you a look that says: "I had to set the tone first. It's just etiquette."
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has to defuse the dog every time the doorbell rings — this is a weekly routine for them
variations: Bonus: after the delivery person leaves, the dog barks twice more, just to inform you, "I successfully defended this home today. Treats would be appropriate."

items.sourceNote: 狗狗年紀變大、變慢的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠年輕的時候，跑得比你還快。
你一拿牽繩，牠就衝到門口，眼神告訴你：快點，世界在等我們。

後來有一天，你拿起牽繩，牠慢慢走過來。
再後來，你叫牠，牠抬起頭花了兩秒才反應。

你才發現：原來陪你的這個小朋友，已經比你想像中走得更遠了。

但牠還是會在你回家時站起來，努力搖兩下尾巴。
那份歡迎，從來沒有變慢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡狗狗已經慢慢變老的朋友——這段話他會看完，然後安靜地過去摸牠一下
variations: 延伸版：你開始走得比牠慢一點，停下來等牠。原來這些年，牠也是這樣等你的，只是你那時候沒有察覺。

en.content:
When it was young, it ran faster than you.
The second you picked up the leash, it sprinted to the door, eyes saying: hurry up, the world is waiting for us.

Then one day, you pick up the leash, and it walks over slowly.
Later, you call its name, and it takes two seconds to lift its head.

That's when you realize: this small friend has actually traveled further with you than you noticed.

But it still stands up when you come home, and gives the tail two soft wags.
That welcome — it never slowed down.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog has been getting older quietly — they'll finish reading and walk over to pet theirs
variations: Extended: you start walking a little slower, pausing so it can catch up. Turns out it's been doing the same for you all these years — you just didn't notice back then.

items.sourceNote: 外星人視角看遛狗的觀察句改寫，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
如果現在有外星人飛過來地球，看到一個畫面：

一個人類，手上拿著繩子。
繩子的另一端，是一隻小型生物。
那個人類，正彎下腰、用塑膠袋把小生物的便便撿起來。

外星人會默默把報告寫成這樣：

「地球的主導物種，已經確認。
體型較小、毛茸茸、不付房租的那一種。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天認真撿便的毛主人——他會邊苦笑邊承認這份報告寫得太準
variations: 進階版：外星人離開前會補一句：「並且那個小生物，會用一個眼神，命令較大的物種改變散步路線。權力結構非常清楚。」

en.content:
If aliens flew to Earth right now, here's the first scene they'd witness:

A human, holding a rope.
On the other end of the rope, a small creature.
The human is bending over, using a plastic bag to pick up the small creature's poop.

The aliens would file this report:

"Dominant species confirmed.
It's the smaller, fluffier one. The one not paying rent."
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who dutifully picks up after their dog — they'll wince at how accurate the report is
variations: Bonus line in the report: 'And the small creature can change the larger one's walking route with a single look. The hierarchy here is unambiguous.'

items.sourceNote: 狗主人家規逐步退讓的觀察改寫，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗第一天的家規：

第一條：狗狗不能上沙發。

養狗第三十天的家規：

第一條：狗狗可以上沙發，但不能上床。

養狗第一百天的家規：

第一條：狗狗可以上床，但要睡腳邊。

養狗第三百天的家規：

第一條：人類睡覺前要先確認狗狗在哪一邊，再決定自己睡哪裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養狗一年以上、家規早就重寫過好幾次的朋友——他會點頭笑說「我家現在已經到第四條了」
variations: 終極版：第三百六十五天，家規只剩下一條——狗狗開心就好。然後人類安靜地搬了一個小床墊到地上。

en.content:
House rules, day one:

Rule one: the dog stays off the couch.

House rules, day thirty:

Rule one: the dog can be on the couch, but not on the bed.

House rules, day one hundred:

Rule one: the dog can be on the bed, but only at the foot.

House rules, day three hundred:

Rule one: before going to sleep, the human checks where the dog is, and then decides which side they get.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner past the one-year mark whose house rules have been quietly rewritten several times — they'll laugh and admit they're already on version four
variations: Final form: by day 365, only one rule survives — the dog is happy. Then the human quietly moves a small mattress onto the floor.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗吃醋情緒觀察改寫，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你在路上摸了別的狗。

你以為這是一件小事。
你以為牠不會發現。
你以為你回家洗手就沒事。

但牠在門口聞到你身上的味道，整隻狗的氣場立刻變了。
牠不看你。牠走到角落，背對著你坐下。
你叫牠，牠耳朵動一下，但頭不轉。

那不是賭氣，那是一份正式的書面抗議。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些「忍不住摸到別的狗、回家被自己家狗冷處理」的朋友——這個情境他這個月已經發生三次
variations: 進階版：你拿出零食試圖和解，牠會先吃，但吃完繼續背對著你。這代表：賄賂收下了，但這件事還沒過。

en.content:
You petted another dog on the street.

You thought it was no big deal.
You thought your dog wouldn't notice.
You thought washing your hands at home would cover it.

But at the door, your dog catches the scent on you, and the entire energy of the room shifts.
It doesn't look at you. It walks to a corner and sits with its back to you.
You call its name. One ear twitches. The head doesn't turn.

That isn't sulking. That's a formal written complaint.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who can't resist petting other dogs and gets the cold shoulder at home — this has happened to them three times this month
variations: Advanced: you offer a treat as a peace offering. The dog eats it. Then turns its back again. The bribe is accepted; the matter is not closed.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗在家工作干擾觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
在家工作 + 養狗 = 一場永遠不會結束的會議干擾。

你開鏡頭，牠走過來舔你的下巴。
你關鏡頭，牠去叼一個會發出尖銳叫聲的玩具。
你拜託牠安靜，牠用更大的力氣咬第二下。
你假裝沒聽到，牠跳上你的椅子。
你終於投降，把牠抱起來——

下一秒，會議的所有同事都看到了一隻狗的下巴。

那一刻，那隻狗成為這場會議的主角。
你只是會議室裡那個拿著鍵盤的助理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天 WFH 都被狗狗插嘴開會的朋友——他的同事早就習慣了那張毛茸茸的臉
variations: 附帶觀察：你的同事們已經先問狗狗好，再問你工作進度。從某個時間點開始，那隻狗的存在感比你更強。

en.content:
Work from home + a dog = a meeting that will be interrupted forever.

Camera on: it walks over and licks your chin.
Camera off: it picks up a squeaky toy.
You beg for quiet: it bites the toy harder, on purpose.
You pretend not to hear: it jumps onto your chair.
You finally surrender and pick it up —

the next second, the entire meeting is staring at a dog's chin.

In that moment, the dog becomes the main character of the call.
You're just the assistant in the room with the keyboard.
en.editorNote: Send to any WFH friend whose dog crashes every meeting — their coworkers have memorized that fluffy face by now
variations: Bonus: your coworkers now greet the dog first, and ask about the project second. At some point, the dog officially out-presenced you on your own calls.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看到行李箱反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是把行李箱從衣櫃裡拿出來。
你還沒打開，你還沒裝任何東西。

但牠已經坐在行李箱旁邊，眼神告訴你三件事：

第一：你不可以走。
第二：如果你硬要走，請把我裝進去。
第三：如果裝不下，那這個行李箱我先躺下。

你最後當然把行程改短了一天。
那不是內疚，那是被一隻狗管理的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次出門前都要被狗狗用眼神挽留的朋友——他知道行李箱根本不是他的
variations: 進階版：你回家那天，行李箱還沒打開，牠已經繞著它走三圈，確認你這次有沒有把牠忘在某個城市。

en.content:
You just pulled the suitcase out of the closet.
You haven't opened it. You haven't packed anything.

But your dog is already sitting next to it, eyes communicating three things:

One: you may not leave.
Two: if you must leave, pack me in.
Three: if I don't fit, then I am claiming this suitcase as a bed.

You ended up shortening the trip by a day.
That's not guilt. That's a life being managed by a small dog.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who gets emotionally negotiated with every time they pull out a suitcase — the suitcase was never theirs
variations: Advanced version: when you come home, the dog circles the unopened suitcase three times — checking whether you accidentally left it in some other city.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗聽到「散步」關鍵字反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，家裡會出現一份「不能說的詞彙清單」：

「散步」要改說「外面活動」。
「洗澡」要改說「水的事情」。
「醫院」要直接拼音、用代號、最好換語言。

但牠永遠比你聰明一步。
你才剛改完代號三天，牠就學會了新代號的意思。

你發現自己每天都在跟一隻狗鬥智，而且常常輸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都在家裡跟狗狗用代號講話的朋友——他家的暗號已經換到第十版
variations: 附帶觀察：你跟另一半在客廳用英文討論「等一下要不要帶牠出去」，牠抬頭看你，眼神說：「我懂英文，下次請說法文。」

en.content:
After you get a dog, your house develops a list of forbidden words:

"Walk" gets replaced with "outside time."
"Bath" becomes "the water thing."
"Vet" gets spelled out, code-named, and ideally translated into another language.

But the dog is always one step ahead.
Three days after you swap in a new code word, it has already learned what it means.

You realize you're in a daily battle of wits with a small animal — and you're losing more often than not.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who speaks in code at home — they're already on version ten of the secret vocabulary
variations: Bonus: you and your partner discuss "taking it out later" in English in the living room. The dog looks up with eyes that say, 'I understand English. Try French next time.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對主人手機嫉妒觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你坐在沙發上滑手機。

第一次：牠走過來，把下巴放在你手機上。
第二次：牠用鼻子把你的手機頂開。
第三次：牠直接坐在你手機跟你的眼睛中間，正面對你。

那個眼神告訴你：「那塊發光的東西不會愛你，我會。」

你最後當然放下手機。
你已經被一隻毛茸茸的生物上完一堂關於陪伴的課。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次滑手機都被狗狗插隊的朋友——他會邊笑邊承認，自己已經被教育很多次了
variations: 進階版：牠發現用爪子撥手機沒用之後，會升級成直接躺到你的鍵盤上，告訴你：「今天的工作到此為止。」

en.content:
You're on the couch scrolling your phone.

First attempt: the dog walks over and rests its chin on your phone.
Second attempt: it noses the phone aside.
Third attempt: it sits directly between your phone and your eyes, facing you.

The look says: "That glowing thing will never love you. I will."

You put the phone down, of course.
A fluffy creature has just delivered a fully formed lecture on presence.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog keeps interrupting their scrolling — they'll laugh and admit they've taken this class many times
variations: Advanced version: after pawing at the phone fails, the dog upgrades to lying directly on your keyboard, gently announcing: 'Work is over for today.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡後暴衝（zoomies）觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了 40 分鐘幫牠洗澡。
你用了三條毛巾。
你蹲在地上吹毛吹到自己的腰快斷掉。

你終於把牠放下來，那一秒鐘，牠變成一顆會飛的毛球：

衝向沙發，鑽進去摩擦。
衝向地毯，全身打滾。
衝向你的床，把臉埋進你的枕頭。

你剛剛洗的那 40 分鐘，正在被牠用 40 秒重新格式化。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次幫狗洗澡完都要追著牠跑全家的朋友——他知道這場比賽永遠是平手
variations: 進階版：你終於追到牠的時候，牠已經香味減半、表情得意。牠不是怕水，牠是怕「乾淨」這個概念。

en.content:
You spent 40 minutes giving the dog a bath.
You used three towels.
You crouched on the floor blow-drying it until your back almost gave out.

The second you put it down, it transforms into a flying fluff missile:

Launches at the couch, burrows in for friction.
Launches at the rug, full-body roll.
Launches at your bed, face-plants into your pillow.

The 40 minutes you just spent are being formatted away in 40 seconds.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who chases their freshly bathed dog around the entire apartment — they know this race always ends in a tie
variations: Advanced version: when you finally catch it, half the clean smell is gone and the dog looks deeply pleased. It's not afraid of water — it's afraid of the concept of 'clean.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對吸塵器恐懼觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你家有一個物品，是狗狗一輩子的宿敵。

那個物品不是隔壁的貓。
不是郵差。
不是煙火。

是吸塵器。

你只是把它從櫃子裡拿出來，還沒插電，牠已經退到房間最深處。
你按下開關，牠開始用吠叫表達牠對這個工業時代的不滿。
你關掉電源，牠走出來，繞著吸塵器走三圈，確認那個惡魔已經被你制服。

從那天起，牠相信你是可以打敗怪獸的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次打掃都要先安撫狗狗的朋友——他家的吸塵器是「重大事件」
variations: 附帶觀察：吸塵器收起來之後，牠會多看你一眼，那個眼神是「下次我可能還是會怕，但今天你贏了」。

en.content:
Every house has one object the dog will hate forever.

It's not the neighbor's cat.
It's not the mailman.
It's not fireworks.

It's the vacuum cleaner.

You pull it out of the closet — not even plugged in — and the dog retreats to the furthest room.
You flip the switch, and it starts barking out a full protest against the industrial age.
You turn it off, and the dog walks out, circles the vacuum three times, and confirms the demon has been subdued.

From that day on, it believes you can defeat monsters.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has to do a pre-cleaning emotional briefing with their dog — the vacuum is a Major Event in that house
variations: Bonus: after you put the vacuum away, the dog gives you one more look. It says, 'I'll probably still be scared next time, but today you won.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗在草地打滾觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你帶牠去公園。
你說：「乖喔，不要弄髒。」

第三秒，牠看到一塊草地。
第四秒，牠的眼神變了。
第五秒，牠四腳朝天，整個身體在草上扭動，像是這片草地會在三秒後消失，牠必須現在就把它的味道全部佔為己有。

你大喊：「不要！」
牠抬頭看你一眼，那個眼神是：「我聽見了，但這件事比你重要。」

你知道你今天回家要再洗一次澡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次帶狗去公園都會看到「全身打滾儀式」的朋友——他已經放棄阻止，只專心拍照
variations: 進階版：草地之後，牠還會去找一塊看起來「味道更深」的地方，告訴你：「這個更好，我要升級。」

en.content:
You take the dog to the park.
You say, "be good, don't get dirty."

Second three: it spots a patch of grass.
Second four: the eyes change.
Second five: it's on its back, full-body wriggle, as if the grass will disappear in three seconds and it must claim every smell right now.

You yell, "NO!"
It looks up at you, and the look says: "I heard you. This is more important."

You already know — another bath when you get home.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who watches the full-body grass ritual every time — they gave up stopping it and now just take photos
variations: Advanced version: after the grass, the dog hunts for a spot with 'deeper notes' and informs you: 'This one is better. I'm upgrading.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗偷襪子觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，你家會出現一個未解之謎：

襪子——永遠少一隻。

你檢查洗衣機，沒有。
你翻沙發底下，沒有。
你問家人，大家都說沒看到。

直到某天，你在牠的小窩深處，挖出一個只有牠知道的「私人收藏品博物館」。

裡面有：
你三隻不同顏色的襪子、一條鞋帶、一張你以為丟掉的發票，
以及一隻你三個月前就放棄找的耳機。

那不是壞習慣，那是牠對你「無聲的紀念」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡常常莫名少東西的毛主人——他的狗狗其實是一位收藏家
variations: 附帶觀察：當你發現那個秘密收藏，牠會走過來，把頭靠在你腳邊，意思是：「謝謝你這幾年提供這麼多紀念品。」

en.content:
After you get a dog, your house develops one unsolved mystery:

Socks. There is always one missing.

You check the washing machine. Nothing.
You look under the couch. Nothing.
You ask your family. No one's seen it.

Until one day, deep inside the dog bed, you discover a small private museum of personal artifacts.

Inventory:
Three of your socks in different colors, one shoelace, a receipt you thought you'd thrown out,
and a single earbud you gave up on three months ago.

That's not a bad habit. That's the dog's silent tribute to you.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose belongings keep disappearing — their dog is, secretly, a curator
variations: Bonus: when you find the secret collection, the dog walks over and rests its head against your foot. The message is: 'Thank you for all these years of fine donations.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗開門進出循環觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
狗狗對門的關係很複雜。

你開門讓牠出去，牠出去三秒，回頭看你，要進來。
你開門讓牠進來，牠進來三秒，看著外面，又想出去。
你再開門，牠走到門口，停下來，思考人生。
你放棄關門，蹲下來等牠。

牠這時候轉身去喝水。

那扇門，已經不是一扇門了。
那是牠每天用來確認你會不會配合的「忠誠度測驗」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天在開門關門中度過下午的朋友——他已經是這場測驗的常駐受試者
variations: 進階版：等你終於坐回沙發，牠又走到門口，回頭看你。第十一回合，開始。

en.content:
A dog's relationship with the door is complicated.

You open the door to let it out — it goes out for three seconds, turns around, wants back in.
You open the door to let it back in — it comes in for three seconds, stares outside, wants out again.
You open it once more — it walks to the threshold, stops, and contemplates life.
You give up on closing the door and crouch down to wait.

It turns around and goes to drink water.

That door isn't a door anymore.
It's a daily loyalty test the dog runs to confirm you'll keep cooperating.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose afternoon is just opening and closing a door — they are a permanent participant in this experiment
variations: Advanced version: the moment you finally sit back down on the couch, the dog returns to the door and looks back at you. Round eleven begins.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對掉地零食的反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你切水果，不小心切到手指。
你「啊」了一聲。

家人在客廳：沒反應。
伴侶在房間：沒反應。
你家狗狗在三公尺外的沙發上：

0.3 秒內衝到廚房，眼睛閃閃發亮。

牠不是來關心你的。
牠是來確認：那個「掉到地上的東西」，現在是不是可以登記在牠名下。

你才發現：真正的家庭成員警報系統，是用「食物」校準的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次手一滑、狗狗就出現的朋友——他家的食物雷達比 Wi-Fi 還靈敏
variations: 進階版：等你包好 OK 繃，牠會用一臉失望的表情看你，意思是：「所以那塊水果呢？沒有就早點說。」

en.content:
You're cutting fruit. You nick your finger.
You let out a small "ouch."

Family in the living room: no reaction.
Partner in the bedroom: no reaction.
Your dog, three meters away on the couch:

in the kitchen in 0.3 seconds, eyes glowing.

It didn't come to check on you.
It came to confirm whether the thing that just hit the floor is now legally claimable.

That's when you realize: the real family alert system in this house runs on food, not feelings.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog teleports the second something hits the floor — that food radar is faster than the Wi-Fi
variations: Advanced version: after you bandage your finger, the dog gives you a deeply disappointed look. Translation: 'So where's the fruit? Next time, lead with that.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗半夜守門巡邏觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，你被一聲低吠叫醒。

你瞇眼看門口，沒人。
你打開窗戶聽外面，沒事。
你問狗狗發生什麼事，牠看你一眼，繼續趴回原位。

你開始懷疑自己是不是聽錯了。
你才剛闔上眼，牠又「汪」一聲。

你才理解：牠不是在叫什麼東西，
牠只是想讓你知道，這個家在牠值班的時候，一切都好。

你雖然失眠了，但你也安心了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有「夜班警衛狗」的朋友——他每晚都被這份免費的安心叫醒
variations: 延伸版：你想罵牠安靜一點，但看到牠耳朵還豎著、眼睛還盯著走廊，你就把那句話吞回去了。有些保護，是用睡眠換的。

en.content:
3 a.m. You're woken up by a low bark.

You squint at the door — no one's there.
You crack the window — nothing outside.
You ask the dog what happened. It glances at you and lies back down.

You start to wonder if you imagined it.
The second your eyes close, another small "woof."

Then you understand: it isn't barking at anything.
It's just letting you know that on its watch, the house is fine.

You lost sleep. But you also relaxed.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog runs the night shift — they get woken up by free peace of mind every single night
variations: Extended: you almost tell it to be quiet, but you see the ears still up, the eyes still locked on the hallway. You swallow the sentence. Some protection costs you sleep.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對玩具偏好的迷之選擇觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了 800 塊買了一個進口的高級狗玩具。

上面有香味、有聲音、有不同顏色的咬咬區。
包裝寫著：「99% 的狗狗最愛。」

你拆開、放在地上，期待牠的反應。

牠走過來，聞一下，然後直接把旁邊一個塑膠袋當成寶藏，叼到角落瘋狂咬。

你坐在沙發上反省人生。
那 800 塊，你應該拿去買 800 個塑膠袋的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次幫狗狗精選玩具、結果牠都選垃圾的朋友——他知道狗狗的審美自成一派
variations: 附帶觀察：那個 800 塊的玩具，三天後還在原位。塑膠袋已經被牠收進私人收藏館，地位等同國寶。

en.content:
You spent $30 on a premium imported dog toy.

It's scented, it squeaks, it has color-coded chew zones.
The packaging says "99% of dogs love this."

You unwrap it, put it on the floor, and wait for the reaction.

The dog walks over, sniffs it once, then grabs a random plastic bag next to it and runs to a corner to chew with full devotion.

You sit on the couch and reflect on your life.
That $30 should have been 30 plastic bags instead.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who curates toys for their dog only to lose to a piece of trash every time — the dog has its own aesthetic and you are not invited
variations: Bonus: the $30 toy is still in the same spot three days later. The plastic bag has been promoted to the private collection, and is now treated as a national treasure.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗陪伴病中的主人觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你發燒了，整個人縮在被子裡。

牠走到床邊，跳上來，沒有像平常那樣翻肚子要摸。
牠安靜地走到你旁邊，把頭靠在你手上。
然後就不動了。

你滑手機，牠陪你滑。
你睡著，牠陪你睡。
你半夜醒來，牠的頭還在你手上。

牠不會說話，所以牠用「不離開」這件事告訴你：
你不是一個人在生病。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給生病時被毛孩照顧過的朋友——他會看完，輕輕摸一下旁邊的牠
variations: 延伸版：你退燒那天，牠才終於放鬆，跳下床去喝水。牠不是不口渴，牠是不想離開。

en.content:
You're running a fever, curled up under the blanket.

The dog walks to the bed, climbs up, but doesn't roll over for belly rubs like usual.
It quietly walks over, rests its head on your hand,
and stops moving.

You scroll your phone — it scrolls with you.
You fall asleep — it falls asleep with you.
You wake up in the middle of the night — its head is still on your hand.

It can't speak. So it uses one fact to tell you everything:
you aren't sick alone.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who's been quietly looked after by their dog when sick — they'll finish reading and softly touch the one beside them
variations: Extended: the day your fever breaks, the dog finally relaxes and jumps off the bed to drink water. It wasn't not thirsty — it just didn't want to leave.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對冰箱開門聲反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你家狗狗的耳朵，是專門為一個聲音設計的：

冰箱門打開的「啵」一聲。

你在房間滑手機，牠睡得像石頭。
你下床走過客廳，牠連眼皮都沒抬。
你走到廚房，打開冰箱——

0.5 秒，牠已經坐在你腳邊。
眼神告訴你：「我也想參與這個重要的決定。」

你只是想拿一杯水。
但你看到那個眼神，最後還是切了一片火腿分給牠。

冰箱的門，是牠每天最期待的開場白。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次開冰箱都會召喚出狗狗的朋友——他家的冰箱聲，比門鈴還有效
variations: 進階版：你假裝開冰箱再關上，想測試牠是不是真的這麼靈。牠會用一個受傷的眼神告訴你：「不要拿這種事情開玩笑。」

en.content:
Your dog's ears are tuned specifically for one sound in this house:

the little "pop" of the fridge door opening.

You're in the bedroom on your phone — it's sleeping like a rock.
You get up and walk through the living room — not a single eyelid moves.
You reach the kitchen, open the fridge —

0.5 seconds later, it's sitting at your feet.
The eyes say: "I would like to participate in this important decision."

You only wanted a glass of water.
But after that look, you end up slicing off a piece of ham to share.

The fridge door is the favorite opening line of its day.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog gets summoned by the fridge — that pop sound is more effective than any doorbell
variations: Advanced version: you fake-open the fridge and close it, just to test the radar. The dog gives you a deeply wounded look that says, 'Please don't joke about things like this.'

items.sourceNote: 幽靈牽繩現象觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你今天沒帶狗狗出門。

但走到一半，你的右手卻自動垂在大腿旁邊。
手指微微彎著，像是握著一條根本不存在的牽繩。

你停下來看自己的手，才發現：
那條繩子，已經跟你的身體合而為一了。

你以為是你在牽牠。
其實這幾年，是牠在牽你的右手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次自己出門都覺得「少了什麼」的毛主人——他的右手早就有自己的記憶
variations: 延伸版：你還會本能地放慢腳步、回頭看後面有沒有人跟上。明明今天牠在家，但你的腳已經習慣了那個節奏。

en.content:
You went out today without your dog.

But halfway down the street, your right hand drops to your side on its own.
Fingers slightly curled, as if gripping a leash that isn't there.

You stop and look at your hand, and finally notice:
that leash has merged with your body.

You thought you were the one walking the dog.
Turns out, for years, it's been the one walking your right hand.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who feels like something's missing when they walk alone — their right hand has its own muscle memory
variations: Extended: you also instinctively slow down and glance back to check if anyone's keeping up. The dog is at home today, but your feet have already learned the pace.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗陪上廁所觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是想去上個廁所。

你關上門，三秒後，門縫底下出現一隻毛茸茸的腳。
你假裝沒看到，繼續滑手機。
門外開始傳來小聲的嗚嗚聲，像是有誰在受苦。

你終於投降，把門打開。
牠搖著尾巴走進來，安靜地坐在你腳邊，像是在參加一場重要儀式。

從那天起，你發現自己一個人上廁所反而怪怪的。
那個畫面少了一隻狗，整個畫面就不完整。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次上廁所都被狗狗強制陪同的朋友——他現在獨自上廁所反而會覺得寂寞
variations: 進階版：朋友來家裡借廁所，狗狗也會跟進去。對方出來時表情很微妙，你只能聳肩說：「牠就是這樣，沒有界線。」

en.content:
You just wanted to use the bathroom.

You close the door. Three seconds later, a fluffy paw appears under the gap.
You pretend not to see it and keep scrolling.
A tiny whimpering sound starts up outside, as if someone is genuinely suffering.

You give up and open the door.
The dog walks in, tail wagging, and sits quietly at your feet like it's attending a sacred ceremony.

From that day on, using the bathroom alone feels strange.
The scene is missing a dog, and the whole composition feels incomplete.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog insists on bathroom escort duty — they now feel oddly lonely going alone
variations: Advanced: a friend visits and uses your bathroom — the dog follows them in too. They come out with a complicated expression. You just shrug: 'That's its policy. No boundaries.'

items.sourceNote: 毛孩讓人生變完整的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之前，你以為日子已經過得差不多了。
上班、回家、滑手機、睡覺。一個人住也算自在。

養狗之後，你才發現：
原來門是會被推開的。
原來椅子旁邊是會有人靠過來的。
原來一個動作，會被另一個生命認真盯著看。

牠沒有改變你的人生方向。
牠只是把那些你以為理所當然的空白，安靜地填了起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近剛養狗、開始覺得家裡變不一樣的朋友——這段話會說中他講不出來的那部分
variations: 延伸版：你才理解，原本那個「自在」，其實只是還沒被打擾過。等到有了被打擾的選項，你才會願意被打擾。

en.content:
Before you had a dog, you thought life was pretty much sorted.
Work, home, phone, sleep. Living alone felt fine.

After you got a dog, you realized:
doors are something that get pushed open.
The space next to your chair is something that gets filled.
Every small action gets watched, intently, by another living thing.

The dog didn't redirect your life.
It just quietly filled in all the blank spaces you assumed were supposed to be empty.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who recently adopted a dog and feels their place is different now — this puts the unspoken part into words
variations: Extended: you realize that 'fine being alone' was just 'not yet interrupted.' Once interruption becomes an option, you choose it on purpose.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對視訊那頭聲音的反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你跟媽媽視訊。

你說的話，狗狗在旁邊完全沒反應。
但媽媽從手機那一頭喊了一聲牠的名字——

牠的耳朵立刻豎起來，眼睛開始掃描房間。
牠走到電視前面，然後走到陽台，最後跑到大門口。

牠不懂科技，但牠懂一件事：
那個叫牠名字的人，是這個家曾經給過牠零食的人。

而那個人沒有出現，讓牠困惑了整整一個下午。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次視訊家人、結果狗狗找人找半天的朋友——這個畫面他每週都會重演
variations: 進階版：你掛掉電話之後，牠還會回去陽台再確認一次。確定真的沒有人來，才嘆氣回到你腳邊。

en.content:
You're on a video call with your mom.

Your voice, the dog ignores completely.
But the second your mom calls its name from the other side of the phone —

the ears shoot up. The eyes start scanning the room.
It walks to the TV. Then the balcony. Then runs to the front door.

It doesn't understand technology, but it understands one thing:
the person calling its name has handed it treats in this house before.

And that person never showed up, which leaves it confused for the rest of the afternoon.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who video calls family and watches their dog hunt the apartment afterward — this scene replays weekly
variations: Advanced: even after you hang up, the dog returns to the balcony for one more check. Only after confirming no one is there does it sigh and walk back to your feet.

items.sourceNote: 狗主人在外人面前的滑稽形象觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
如果有外星人飛過台灣的公園，

看到一個人類，
手裡拿著一條繩子，
繩子另一頭有一隻狗，
那隻狗在草地上自由地大便，
然後人類蹲下來，用塑膠袋把它撿起來——

外星人會回去寫報告：

「地球上的高等智慧生物，叫做狗。
人類是他們的助理。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天負責撿大便、卻甘之如飴的飼主——他會邊笑邊承認自己就是助理
variations: 進階版：如果牠在公園拉到不只一坨，外星人會默默調整：「人類不是助理，是隨從。」

en.content:
If an alien spaceship flew over a city park,

they'd see a human,
holding a string,
attached to a dog,
who freely poops on the grass,
while the human kneels down with a plastic bag to pick it up —

the alien would file the report:

"Earth's dominant species is called the dog.
The humans are their assistants."
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who picks up dog poop daily and somehow doesn't mind — they'll laugh and admit they are, in fact, the assistant
variations: Advanced: if the dog produces more than one pile, the alien quietly revises the report: 'Humans are not assistants. They're staff.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對主人能力的盲目崇拜觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
在你家狗狗的眼裡，你是無所不能的。

你會打開零食袋（牠打不開）。
你會控制門（門對牠來說是一道謎）。
你會讓電視出現會動的小生物（牠覺得你是魔法師）。
你會從冰箱拿出香腸（牠覺得冰箱是另一個次元）。

你的人生目標，
從某一天開始悄悄變成：

別讓那個小東西失望。
至少要當一次，牠以為的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近覺得自己一事無成的朋友——他家狗狗會用整個生命告訴他，他超棒
variations: 延伸版：你不一定要是世界第一，但你一定是某一隻狗心目中的世界第一。這件事，沒人能拿走。

en.content:
In your dog's eyes, you can do anything.

You can open the snack bag (it can't).
You control the door (a complete mystery to it).
You make small moving creatures appear on the TV (clearly, magic).
You produce sausage from the fridge (some kind of portal).

At some point,
your life goal quietly becomes:

don't let that little thing down.
At least once, be the person it thinks you are.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone feeling like they haven't accomplished anything lately — their dog has spent every day insisting otherwise
variations: Extended: you don't have to be number one in the world. You're already number one in the world to one specific dog. Nobody can take that away.

items.sourceNote: 米克斯與柴犬個性反差觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
家裡同時養米克斯跟柴犬，是一場每天都在進行的對照實驗。

你叫米克斯：「不可以進廚房。」
牠就坐在門口，眼睛盯著你，一步都不踏進來。
你叫柴犬：「不可以進廚房。」
牠看著你，慢慢走進廚房，停下來，再看你一眼，像在確認你剛剛是不是真的這樣說。

米克斯把規則當合約。
柴犬把規則當建議。
而你，是那個一次又一次重新解釋條款的中間人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡同時有米克斯跟柴犬的朋友——他每天都活在這場條款談判裡
variations: 進階版：訓練了三個月之後，米克斯已經能聽懂十個指令。柴犬學會了一個——但牠選擇不執行。

en.content:
Owning a mixed-breed and a Shiba at the same time is a live comparative study.

You tell the mixed-breed: "Don't come into the kitchen."
It sits at the doorway, eyes locked on you, refusing to take a single step in.
You tell the Shiba: "Don't come into the kitchen."
It stares at you, slowly walks into the kitchen, stops, looks back, as if confirming whether that was really what you just said.

The mixed-breed treats rules as a contract.
The Shiba treats rules as a suggestion.
And you are the middleman who has to renegotiate the terms every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who lives with both a mixed-breed and a Shiba — they negotiate the kitchen treaty daily
variations: Advanced: after three months of training, the mixed-breed understands ten commands. The Shiba has learned one — and chosen not to comply.

items.sourceNote: 狗毛無所不在的家居現實觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，你會發現一件事：

狗毛是一種不需要狗在場，也會持續出現的物質。

牠在沙發上躺過一次，那塊布就再也不會乾淨了。
你穿黑色衣服出門，襯衫上會自動長出三根白毛。
你打開便當蓋子，裡面也會有一根毛——你完全不知道牠是怎麼進去的。

後來你放棄了。
你開始把那一根毛，當作家裡的簽名。
它在哪裡，家就延伸到哪裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天黏滾筒、卻已經放棄追究的飼主——這段話會讓他笑著嘆氣
variations: 延伸版：朋友來家裡，看到他黑色外套上沾了三根毛，露出抱歉的表情。你只能笑說：「沒事，這代表你被認證過了。」

en.content:
After you get a dog, you discover one thing:

dog hair is a substance that continues to appear even when the dog is not present.

It lay on the couch once — that fabric is now permanently coated.
You wear black to go out — three white hairs will spontaneously grow on your shirt.
You open your lunchbox — there is a hair inside, and you have absolutely no idea how it got there.

Eventually, you give up.
You start treating each stray hair like the household signature.
Wherever it lands, that's where home extends to.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has stopped fighting the lint roller war — this will make them laugh and sigh at the same time
variations: Extended: a friend comes over, finds three hairs on their black coat, and looks apologetic. You just smile: 'No worries. That means you've been certified.'

items.sourceNote: 沙發規則與飼主投降的日常觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
「我家狗狗不准上沙發。」

（兩秒後）

「我家狗狗目前在沙發上。」

你不是訓練失敗，你是被一隻 8 公斤的生物溫柔地推翻政權。
牠用的不是武力，是那雙看著你像在說「但是我好可愛欸」的眼睛。
從那一刻起，沙發就不再是你的了。它只是你被允許坐的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有「規則歸規則、狗歸狗」的飼主——他會立刻看一眼自己腳邊的那隻共犯
variations: 進階版：你買了第二張椅子，準備劃清界線。三天後，牠也躺上去了。你才發現——你不是在養狗，你是在跟牠分租房子。

en.content:
"My dog isn't allowed on the couch."

(Two seconds later.)

"My dog is currently on the couch."

You didn't fail at training. You were gently overthrown by an 8-kilogram creature.
It didn't use force. It used those eyes that say, "but I'm so cute though."
From that moment on, the couch was no longer yours. It became the place you were permitted to sit.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose house rules dissolve the moment the dog walks in — they'll glance at the accomplice at their feet
variations: Advanced: you buy a second chair to draw a clean line. Three days later, the dog is on it too. That's when you realize — you're not raising a dog, you're sharing rent with one.

items.sourceNote: 外星人視角看遛狗的荒謬感，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
如果有一天外星人經過地球，看到我們這個星球上的人類——

手裡牽著一條繩子，一頭綁著一隻毛茸茸的生物。
那隻生物在前面跑，人類在後面追。
那隻生物隨地大便，人類蹲下來，用塑膠袋慎重地把它撿起來，再帶回家。

外星人會回到太空船寫報告：
「地球上有兩種生物，一種是主，一種是僕。」
然後牠們會指錯，是哪一種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天清晨拎著便便袋出門的飼主——他會笑著承認自己就是那個僕人
variations: 延伸版：你蹲在路邊撿便便的時候，剛好有路人經過。那一秒你不是飼主，你是地球生態系最謙卑的清潔員。

en.content:
If aliens passed by Earth one day, they'd see something like this:

A human holding a rope. The other end of the rope is tied to a small furry creature.
The creature runs ahead. The human chases after it.
The creature poops on the ground, and the human crouches down, carefully picks it up with a plastic bag, and carries it home.

The aliens would return to their ship and write the report:
"Earth has two species. One is the master, one is the servant."
And they would absolutely get the labels wrong.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who walks their dog at 6 a.m. with a poop bag — they'll laugh and admit which one they are
variations: Extended: you're crouched on the sidewalk picking it up when a stranger walks past. For that one second, you're not a dog owner — you're the most humble janitor in Earth's ecosystem.

items.sourceNote: 米克斯服從度極高的暖心觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
米克斯不是最漂亮的狗，也不是最名貴的狗。

但牠是那種——你蹲下來流眼淚的時候，會默默走過來，把頭靠在你膝蓋上的狗。

牠不會說話，可是牠知道你今天不太好。
牠不會勸你，可是牠會在你旁邊呼吸，慢慢地、穩穩地，像一個無聲的提醒：
你還在這裡，我也還在這裡。

養米克斯的人都懂——牠們的好，不在外觀，在那種一句話都不需要說的陪伴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養米克斯的朋友——他第一段就會紅了眼眶
variations: 進階版：別人問你為什麼不養純種狗，你只會笑笑說：「我家這隻很完美。」其實你想說的是：牠選了我，我也選了牠，這比血統重要太多了。

en.content:
A mixed-breed isn't the prettiest dog. It isn't the most expensive dog.

But it's the kind of dog that — when you crouch down and cry — quietly walks over and rests its head on your knee.

It can't speak. But somehow it knows you're not okay today.
It won't try to fix anything. It just breathes next to you, slowly and steadily, like a silent reminder:
you're still here, and so am I.

Anyone who's lived with a mixed-breed knows — their value isn't in their looks. It's in the kind of company that doesn't need a single word.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who adopted a mutt — they'll get teary by the second line
variations: Advanced: when someone asks why you didn't get a purebred, you just smile and say: "My dog is perfect." What you really mean is: it chose me, and I chose it. That matters more than any pedigree.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對食物聲音的反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你打開零食袋的聲音，全家最遠的房間都聽得到。
你叫牠名字三次，牠裝作沒聽見。
你問牠：「要不要散步？」牠飛奔過來，撞翻你手上的咖啡。
你問牠：「要不要洗澡？」牠瞬間變成一灘融化在地上的奶油。

所以你開始理解一件事：
牠不是聽不懂中文，牠只是聽得太懂了。
牠選擇性失聰，不是因為耳朵壞掉，是因為腦袋太好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天被狗狗選擇性失聰氣到笑出來的飼主——他會直接點頭
variations: 進階版：你發現牠連你還沒拿出零食袋、只是「想」要拿出來的腳步聲，都認得出來。那一刻你才確定——這個家裡，真正掌握資訊的人不是你。

en.content:
The sound of you opening a treat bag is audible from the farthest room in the house.
You call its name three times. It pretends to hear nothing.
You ask, "Wanna go for a walk?" — it sprints over and knocks the coffee out of your hand.
You ask, "Wanna take a bath?" — it instantly melts into a puddle of butter on the floor.

That's when you start to understand:
it's not that it can't understand you. It understands you too well.
The selective deafness isn't a hearing problem. It's a processing decision.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog is fluent in selective deafness — they'll nod before they finish reading
variations: Advanced: you realize it can recognize the footsteps you make when you're just thinking about getting the treat bag — before you've even touched it. That's when you accept that you're not the one running the household.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡瞬間的崩潰反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠平常聽不懂中文。

但只要你嘴巴含著毛巾、手伸向蓮蓬頭，牠瞬間就懂了。

下一秒——
牠用你想像不到的速度，鑽進床底、衣櫃縫、沙發底下那個只有蟑螂去過的地方。
你蹲下去找牠，牠用一種「你居然認得出我」的表情看你。

養狗以後你才知道：
牠的智商從來沒有問題，問題是牠願不願意配合你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次洗澡都要在家裡演一場諜對諜的飼主——他會直接傳回一個「就是這樣」的貼圖
variations: 進階版：牠出來的時候眼神死，毛濕到變一條，全身散發一股「我恨你但我還是愛你」的氣息。然後牠衝到客廳地毯上，開始那場每次洗完澡的瘋狂甩水奔跑儀式。

en.content:
Most days, your dog doesn't understand a word you say.

But the second you put a towel between your teeth and reach for the showerhead, it speaks fluent English.

Next frame —
it teleports under the bed, into a closet gap, behind the sofa where only cockroaches have ever been.
You crouch down and find it, and it stares back like, "I can't believe you recognized me."

That's when you realize:
the dog has never had an IQ problem. It's a willingness problem.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has to run a counter-intelligence operation every bath night — they'll send back a 'this is too accurate' meme
variations: Advanced: it comes out wet, eyes dead, fur reduced to a single strand, radiating a vibe that says 'I hate you and I love you.' Then it sprints to the living room rug for the post-bath zoomies — the sacred ritual every dog signs up for.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗無條件的愛與信任，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠不在意你今天賺多少錢。
牠不在意你穿什麼牌子的衣服。
牠不在意你的房子有沒有電梯、車子是不是新的。

牠只在意——你今天有沒有回家。

你打開門那一刻，對牠來說就是全世界最重要的新聞。
你蹲下來摸牠的頭，對牠來說就是一整天最值得活的理由。

人類花一輩子在追那些很複雜的東西。
狗狗只想要你回家、坐下、把手放在牠頭上。
簡單到有點不公平。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近覺得人生很累的朋友——他讀完會默默打開手機看自家狗的照片
variations: 進階版：你發現自己上班最辛苦的那天，最想趕快回家的不是床，是那雙在門口等你、尾巴快要打結的眼睛。

en.content:
Your dog doesn't care how much you earned today.
It doesn't care what brand you're wearing.
It doesn't care if your apartment has an elevator or your car is new.

It cares about one thing — whether you came home.

The moment you open the door is, to your dog, the most important headline in the world.
You crouch down and pat its head, and that's the reason today was worth living.

Humans spend a lifetime chasing complicated things.
A dog just wants you home, seated, with one hand on its head.
It's almost unfair how simple that is.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been burned out lately — they'll quietly open their phone and stare at a photo of their dog
variations: Advanced: on your worst workday, you realize the thing you're rushing home to isn't the bed. It's the pair of eyes at the door and a tail spinning so fast it's about to tie itself in a knot.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗睡姿與信任感的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
別人家的狗：規矩睡在自己的窩裡。

你家的狗：四腳朝天躺在你床中央，肚皮朝上，舌頭歪一邊，鼾聲比你還大。

你想推牠到旁邊一點——牠用一種「你誰啊」的眼神看你。
你乾脆把自己縮到床角——牠舒服地伸個懶腰，把你剩下的那 30 公分也佔走。

你才意識到：
你不是這張床的主人，你是付房貸的房客。
真正的屋主，現在睡得比你還香。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種半夜被狗踢醒、卻還是捨不得把牠推下床的飼主——他會邊笑邊認帳
variations: 進階版：你終於買了張更大的床，覺得這次總可以兩個人都睡得開了。結果牠用同一招——還是平躺在正中央，把整張床劃成牠的領土，你只是租客升等。

en.content:
Other people's dogs: sleep politely in their own beds.

Your dog: lies belly-up in the dead center of your mattress, tongue lolling to one side, snoring louder than you.

You try to nudge it over — it gives you a look that says, "And you are…?"
You give up and curl into the corner — it stretches out and claims the last 30 centimeters you had left.

That's when it hits you:
you are not the owner of this bed. You are the tenant paying the mortgage.
The real homeowner is currently sleeping better than you are.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who gets kicked awake at 3 a.m. and still can't bring themselves to push the dog off — they'll laugh and confess
variations: Advanced: you finally buy a bigger bed, sure that this time both of you will fit. Same outcome — flat out in the middle, claiming the whole territory. You just got upgraded to a slightly nicer rental.

items.sourceNote: 老狗陪伴歲月的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠以前可以追球追到飛起來。
現在追兩步就喘，但還是會把球叼到你腳邊，眼睛亮亮地看著你。

牠以前聽到「散步」會直接撞翻整個鞋櫃。
現在牠走得比較慢，會在熟悉的轉角停下來嗅一嗅，像在跟這個世界打招呼。

你發現你開始放慢自己的腳步，不是因為趕時間，
是因為你想——多陪牠走一段路，多停一下下。

養老狗才懂：
那不是衰老，是牠在用最後的力氣，把每一天過得很慢、很深、很值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有老狗的朋友——他第二段就會深呼吸一下
variations: 進階版：你會開始把每一次「牠跑過來」都當成禮物，把每一個「牠在你腳邊睡著」都當成限量款。日子變慢以後，你才發現牠陪你的每一秒，原來都很貴。

en.content:
It used to chase a ball so hard it almost flew.
Now it jogs two steps, gets winded, but still drops the ball at your feet, eyes shining.

It used to hear the word "walk" and knock over the whole shoe rack.
Now it walks slower, stops at familiar corners to sniff, like it's saying hello to the world one more time.

You notice you've started slowing your own pace too — not because you're tired,
but because you want to walk a little farther with it. Stop a little longer.

That's what living with a senior dog teaches you:
this isn't decline. It's your dog using its last reserves of strength to live each day slowly, deeply, fully.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog is getting old — they'll need a quiet breath after the second paragraph
variations: Advanced: you start treating every 'it ran toward me' moment as a gift, every 'it fell asleep at my feet' as a limited edition. When time slows down, you finally realize how expensive each second with your dog actually is.

items.sourceNote: 狗主人會幫狗取一堆綽號的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠的身分證上叫「Lucky」。

但日常生活中，牠的名字包括：
寶貝、小屁股、毛球、傻蛋、肚皮先生、牛肉乾大盜、三秒就忘記主人的笨狗、還有「欸你」。

每一個綽號都對應一個情境：
罵牠的時候叫全名，逗牠玩的時候叫毛球，半夜偷舔你臉的時候叫小屁股，看到牠睡翻過去就叫肚皮先生。

養狗的人才懂：
一隻狗起碼要有五個綽號，才算是家人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種會用十幾種語氣叫狗名字的朋友——他立刻就會開始在心裡列清單
variations: 進階版：你每換一個綽號，狗就用一種不同的眼神回應你。叫全名牠裝沒聽見，叫毛球牠尾巴搖三下，叫「欸你」牠頭都不抬——因為牠知道，這個綽號代表你只是想看看牠在不在。

en.content:
Its ID says "Lucky."

But in daily life, its names include:
baby, little butt, fluffball, dummy, Mr. Belly, the beef-jerky bandit, the dog who forgets you in three seconds, and "hey you."

Each nickname has its own purpose:
full name when you're scolding, fluffball when you're playing, little butt when it licks your face at 2 a.m., Mr. Belly when it's passed out upside down on the couch.

Dog owners know the rule:
a dog needs at least five nicknames before it officially counts as family.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who calls their dog by a different name in every tone of voice — they'll start mentally listing their own
variations: Advanced: each nickname gets a different reaction. Full name? It pretends not to hear. Fluffball? Three tail wags. "Hey you"? It doesn't even look up — because it knows that one just means you wanted to check it was still there.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡時間像犯罪現場的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是輕輕說了一句：「我們來洗澡囉。」

下一秒，狗狗已經消失。

你在沙發底下找到一個尾巴。
你在棉被裡找到一個頭。
你在門後找到一個閉著眼睛、假裝自己是隱形人的牠。

等你終於把牠抱進浴室——
牠四肢張開、卡在門框、眼神死，活像剛被警察抓到的逃犯。

洗完澡，牠用全身的力氣甩水，把整個浴室還給你，再用受害者的眼神看你三十分鐘。

養狗才懂：
洗澡不是清潔，是雙方各退一步的和平協議。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個被狗狗在洗澡前演出生離死別的飼主——他第一段就會笑出聲
variations: 進階版：洗完之後牠在你床上瘋狂打滾，把整張床當毛巾用——你才意識到，剛剛那場 30 分鐘的戰爭，其實是牠贏了。

en.content:
You softly say, "Time for a bath."

A second later, the dog has vanished.

You find a tail under the couch.
A head buried in the blanket.
The rest of it behind the door, eyes closed, pretending to be invisible.

When you finally drag it into the bathroom —
legs braced against the doorframe, dead eyes, the exact look of a fugitive caught on camera.

After the bath, it uses every muscle in its body to shake the water back into your face, then stares at you for thirty straight minutes like a wronged victim.

Only dog owners understand:
bath time isn't cleaning. It's a peace treaty where both sides lose a little.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who lives through this exact 30-minute hostage situation every weekend — they'll laugh by line two
variations: Advanced: as soon as it's clean, it rolls across your bed using the entire mattress as a towel. That's when you realize — that 30-minute war? You didn't win. You just thought you did.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬戲精性格的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
別的狗：草卡牙縫，自己舔一舔就好。

柴犬：草卡牙縫，當場躺平，眼神空洞，發出垂死的悲鳴，像是在告別這個殘酷的世界。

你蹲下來看，發現只是一根 0.5 公分的草。
你把它拿掉。
牠立刻原地復活，搖著尾巴跑去吃飯，把剛剛那場感人的訣別演出忘得一乾二淨。

養柴犬才懂：
你不是飼主，你是這齣連續劇的觀眾兼編劇兼救世主。
而且每一集，牠都拿影帝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有柴犬的朋友——他會邊笑邊回想昨天又被牠演了哪一齣
variations: 進階版：你拿掉那根草以後，牠用一種「我以為這次真的回不來了」的眼神看你三秒，然後若無其事跳上沙發。你才意識到——剛剛那場戲，主角其實是你。

en.content:
Other dogs: blade of grass stuck in teeth — they lick it off and move on.

Shiba Inu: blade of grass stuck in teeth — flops to the floor, dead eyes, lets out a dying whimper, says farewell to this cruel world.

You crouch down and find a single half-centimeter strand of grass.
You remove it.
It instantly resurrects, wags its tail, runs off to eat dinner, and forgets the entire tragic farewell ever happened.

Shiba owners understand:
you're not the pet parent. You're the audience, the screenwriter, and the savior of an ongoing soap opera.
And every episode, your dog wins the Best Actor award.
en.editorNote: Send to any Shiba owner — they'll instantly recall yesterday's Oscar-worthy performance over absolutely nothing
variations: Advanced: after you remove the grass, it stares at you for three seconds with the look of someone who genuinely thought they wouldn't make it back, then casually hops onto the couch. That's when you realize — the real protagonist of that scene was you.

items.sourceNote: 狗毛無所不在的飼主經驗，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之前，你在意衣服上有沒有毛球。

養狗之後，你的黑色外套是灰的，白色襯衫是淺米色的，咖啡裡偶爾漂著一根，便當夾出一根你也只是說一聲：「喔，是牠的。」然後繼續吃。

你出門前用滾筒滾五次，到公司還是有同事默默幫你拔掉肩膀那一根。
你不再解釋，只是笑著說：「對，我有狗。」

養狗才懂：
狗毛不是髒，是你身上一輩子都洗不掉的標籤——
上面寫著：這個人，被一隻毛茸茸的生物深深愛著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個衣櫃裡的衣服全部變成「狗毛色」的朋友——他會邊笑邊看一眼自己今天的褲子
variations: 進階版：有一天你穿了一件完全沒毛的衣服出門，反而覺得哪裡怪怪的——好像少了什麼。回家看到牠在沙發上翻滾，你才安心：嗯，標籤還在。

en.content:
Before getting a dog, you cared whether your clothes had lint.

After getting a dog, your black jacket is grey, your white shirt is light beige, there's occasionally a strand floating in your coffee, and when you find one in your lunchbox you just say, "Oh, that's hers," and keep eating.

You lint-roll five times before leaving the house, and a coworker still quietly picks one off your shoulder. You stop explaining. You just smile and say, "Yeah, I have a dog."

Only dog people understand:
dog hair isn't dirt. It's a permanent label you wear forever —
and it reads: this human is deeply loved by something fluffy.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend whose entire wardrobe is now "dog-fur colored" — they'll laugh and glance down at today's pants
variations: Advanced: one day you leave the house in a completely lint-free outfit and feel weirdly off, like something's missing. You get home, see your dog rolling on the couch, and relax — good, the label's still there.

items.sourceNote: 家規從人本變成狗本的演化過程，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗第一個月：「不准上沙發。」

第三個月：「沙發可以，但床不行。」

第六個月：「床可以，但被子下面不行。」

第一年：「⋯⋯你睡中間，我擠邊邊就好。」

第二年：你已經弓起身體睡成一個半圓，把整張床三分之二讓給一隻 8 公斤的生物。牠打呼，你忍著。牠翻身踢你的腰，你說：「不好意思，我不該擋到。」

養狗才懂：
家裡的階級，是從你失守的那一刻開始重組的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給任何一個半夜會被狗踢醒、卻還幫狗蓋被子的朋友——他自己也搞不清楚是怎麼變這樣的
variations: 進階版：有一天你終於跟伴侶說：「我們是不是該訓練牠睡狗窩？」狗在旁邊抬頭看你一眼，你立刻補一句：「⋯⋯改天再說。」

en.content:
Month one of dog ownership: "No dogs on the couch."

Month three: "Okay, the couch, but not the bed."

Month six: "Fine, the bed, but not under the covers."

Year one: "...You take the middle, I'll squeeze onto the edge."

Year two: you're sleeping curled into a half-moon shape, giving up two-thirds of your mattress to an 8-kilogram creature. It snores. You endure. It rolls over and kicks you in the ribs. You whisper, "Sorry, didn't mean to be in the way."

Only dog people understand:
the household power structure quietly reorganizes the moment you give up the first inch.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who gets kicked awake at 3 a.m. and still tucks the dog back in — they have no idea how it came to this
variations: Advanced: one day you tell your partner, "Maybe we should train them to sleep in the dog bed?" The dog lifts its head and looks at you. You immediately add, "...maybe next month."

items.sourceNote: 狗的無條件接納帶來的療癒感，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
今天你在公司被主管念，搭捷運被擠到崩潰，回家路上想了三十種辭職理由。

你打開家門。

牠不問你今天賺了多少、有沒有升遷、KPI 達標了沒。
牠只在乎一件事：你回來了。

牠用全身撞你的腿，尾巴搖到自己歪一邊，眼睛裡只有你。

你蹲下來抱牠，眼眶有點熱。
你想：原來世界上還有一個地方，
不需要你變得更好，就願意全力歡迎你。

那個地方，剛好有四隻腳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近過得很累的朋友——他不一定會回覆，但他會默默存下來
variations: 進階版：你抱完牠站起來，牠立刻跑去叼自己的玩具給你——好像在說：你今天累了，這個給你玩，會比較開心。

en.content:
Today your boss chewed you out, the train home was a sweaty mess, and on the walk back you mentally drafted thirty different resignation letters.

You open the front door.

It doesn't ask how much you earned, whether you got promoted, or if you hit your KPIs.
It only cares about one thing: you came home.

It body-slams your legs, wags its tail so hard it tilts sideways, looks at you like you're the only thing in the room.

You crouch down, hold it, and your eyes get a little warm.
You think: somewhere in this world, there is still a place
that doesn't ask you to be better — it just fully welcomes you in.

And that place happens to have four legs.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been having a rough stretch — they may not reply, but they'll quietly save it
variations: Advanced: as you stand up, it runs off and brings back its favorite toy — as if to say, you had a hard day, take this, it'll help.

items.sourceNote: 狗對食物的執著行為觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你在客廳剝了一根香蕉。

剝皮的聲音才響 0.3 秒，
牠從房間裡用滑壘的方式出現在你面前，
眼睛瞪得像剛中樂透，
表情寫著：「我願意用三年壽命換一口。」

你說：「狗不能吃太多香蕉。」
牠：（眼神更悲傷）
你：「⋯⋯好啦一小塊。」
牠：（瞬間振作，嚼兩下吞下去，然後繼續看著你，像剛剛什麼都沒發生）

養狗才懂：
你以為你在馴服一隻狗，
其實是一隻狗在用十年時間，把你訓練成一台會自動發放零食的機器。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個只要打開冰箱、狗就出現在腳邊的朋友——他立刻就有畫面
variations: 進階版：有一天你只是把保鮮盒從架上拿下來，牠就從另一個房間跑來坐好——你那一刻才意識到，牠可能比你還清楚你冰箱裡有什麼。

en.content:
You peel a banana in the living room.

The sound of the peel lasts 0.3 seconds.
It slides out of the bedroom like a baseball player going for home plate,
eyes wide like it just won the lottery,
face clearly saying, "I would trade three years of my life for one bite."

You say, "Dogs shouldn't have too much banana."
It: (sadder eyes)
You: "...okay, one tiny piece."
It: (instantly recovers, chews twice, swallows, and stares at you again like nothing happened)

Only dog people understand:
you think you're training a dog.
The dog has actually spent ten years training you into an automatic snack-dispensing machine.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend whose dog appears at their feet the second the fridge opens — they'll instantly picture it
variations: Advanced: one day you just pull a tupperware off the shelf, and your dog comes running from another room and sits politely. That's when you realize — it knows what's in your fridge better than you do.

items.sourceNote: 狗活在當下的智慧帶給人的啟發，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你坐在沙發上，腦子轉著：
下週的提案、月底的房租、十年後的退休、要不要再進修一個學位。

你抬頭看牠。

牠正在認真研究地上一道陽光。
陽光移動了 5 公分，牠也跟著移動 5 公分。
躺下，瞇眼，深深嘆一口氣，那種「我很滿足」的嘆氣。

那一刻你發現：
你花了三十年讀書、工作、煩惱，
才終於開始思考「人生的意義」這件事。

而牠用三秒鐘，在那道陽光裡，找到了。

養狗才懂：
有時候你不是在養牠，
是牠在用最簡單的方式，提醒你怎麼活。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近想很多、卻睡不太著的朋友——他看完會抬頭看一眼自家的狗
variations: 進階版：你跟著牠一起躺到那道陽光裡。牠側頭看你三秒，好像在說：你終於懂了。然後又閉上眼睛。

en.content:
You're on the couch, mind spinning:
next week's pitch, this month's rent, retirement in ten years, whether to go back for another degree.

You look up at your dog.

It's seriously studying a patch of sunlight on the floor.
The sunlight shifts five centimeters. It shifts five centimeters too.
Lies down, squints, lets out a deep, satisfied sigh — that "I have everything I need" kind of sigh.

In that moment you realize:
you spent thirty years studying, working, worrying,
just to finally start asking what the meaning of life is.

It found the answer in three seconds, inside a square of light on the floor.

Only dog people understand:
sometimes you're not the one taking care of them.
They are quietly, in the simplest possible way, showing you how to live.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been overthinking and not sleeping — they'll look up at their dog after reading
variations: Advanced: you lie down in that same square of sunlight. Your dog tilts its head, looks at you for three seconds as if to say, finally, you got it — then closes its eyes again.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗在沙發上佔位、人類只能租用一角的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你買了一張兩人座沙發。

第一週：你坐中間，牠縮在一角。
第二週：你往邊邊靠，把中間讓給牠。
第三週：你坐扶手，腿懸空，因為牠把全身攤平。
第四週：你直接坐地板，邊滑手機邊抬頭問牠：「你還好嗎？」

養狗才懂：
房子是你買的，沙發是你刷的，房貸是你扛的——
但這個家真正的房東，只有四隻腳，從來不繳租。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個跟狗搶沙發、永遠輸到笑出來的朋友——他立刻看一眼自家那位地主
variations: 進階版：你終於忍不住把牠抱起來想坐回中間，牠在你大腿上重新攤平，再嘆一口氣。你瞬間明白，剛剛只是換了個方式繼續輸。

en.content:
You bought a two-seater sofa.

Week 1: You sit in the middle, your dog curls up in a corner.
Week 2: You slide to the edge so your dog can have the middle.
Week 3: You're balanced on the armrest, legs dangling, because your dog has gone full starfish.
Week 4: You're on the floor, scrolling your phone, glancing up to ask, "You doing okay up there?"

Only dog people understand:
you bought the house, you swiped the card for the couch, you carry the mortgage —
but the real landlord of this place has four legs and has never paid rent in its life.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who keeps losing the couch war and laughing about it — they'll glance over at their tiny landlord
variations: Advanced: you finally scoop the dog up to reclaim the middle. It re-flops across your lap, sighs deeply, and you realize you just lost the same battle in a different costume.

items.sourceNote: 狗對主人的歡迎沒有條件、不評分的特質，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
人會記帳。

你上禮拜遲回三次訊息、上個月忘了一個生日、
去年那場聚會你說錯了一句話——
有人都還記得，可能還會再提一次。

但牠不會。

你早上對牠兇了一下，下午回家，牠還是用同一張臉迎接你。
你出差五天，回來牠一秒原諒你。
你心情不好把自己關房間，牠就趴在門口等到你願意出來。

養狗才懂：
世界上有一種愛，不會分期、不會結算、不會翻舊帳。
那種愛剛好住在你家，每天早上用鼻子把你撞醒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近覺得自己「不夠好」的朋友——他會抬頭看一眼那位從不記仇的室友
variations: 進階版：你跟牠道歉「早上對你兇，對不起」。牠歪頭看你三秒，然後把頭靠在你膝蓋上——好像在說：我早就忘了，是你還記得。

en.content:
Humans keep score.

You were slow to reply three times last week, you forgot one birthday last month,
you said the wrong thing at that dinner last year —
someone still remembers, and may bring it up again.

Your dog doesn't.

You snapped at it this morning. You come home in the afternoon, and you get the same face you always get.
You're gone five days for work — you're forgiven in one second.
You shut yourself in your room because you're sad, and it just lies down outside the door until you're ready.

Only dog people understand:
there's a kind of love that doesn't run a tab, doesn't settle accounts, doesn't bring up the past.
That love happens to live in your house, and wakes you up every morning by booping you with its nose.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's feeling like they're not enough — they'll glance over at the roommate who has never once kept score
variations: Advanced: you tell your dog, "sorry for snapping at you this morning." It tilts its head, looks at you for three seconds, then rests its head on your knee — as if to say, I already forgot, you're the one still holding it.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對拆包裹聲音的執著反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你拆一個網購紙箱。

膠帶被撕開的那 0.5 秒——
牠從房間衝出來，腳底打滑撞牆，
眼睛閃閃發亮，尾巴搖到變成風扇，
表情寫著：「是給我的吧？一定是給我的吧？」

你拿出來的是衛生紙。

牠：（深深嘆氣，慢慢轉身，回房間躺下，背影寫著「我不想跟你說話」）

十分鐘後你拆下一個箱子。
牠又衝出來。

養狗才懂：
牠不是健忘，牠是永遠相信下一個箱子會是奇蹟。
而我們花了一輩子，都還沒學會這種樂觀。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個家裡有「拆箱嗅探犬」的朋友——他會立刻聽見自家狗那聲嘆氣
variations: 進階版：那箱衛生紙你最後留了一張紙箱蓋給牠當枕頭。牠枕了三秒，又跑來看你拆下一箱——希望從未熄滅。

en.content:
You start opening a delivery box.

In the 0.5 seconds it takes to peel back the tape —
your dog comes flying out of the bedroom, skids into the wall,
eyes lit up like a slot machine, tail spinning into a fan,
face clearly saying, "That's for me, right? That has to be for me, right?"

What you pull out is a pack of toilet paper.

Dog: (deep sigh, slow turn, walks back to the bedroom, lies down, the back of its head saying "I'm not speaking to you right now")

Ten minutes later, you open the next box.
It sprints out again.

Only dog people understand:
it's not forgetfulness — it just believes, every single time, that the next box might be the miracle.
We've spent our whole lives and still haven't learned to be that optimistic.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend whose dog acts as the household package inspector — they'll hear that sigh in their head
variations: Advanced: you save a flap of cardboard from the toilet paper box and give it to your dog as a pillow. It rests its chin on it for three seconds, then comes back to supervise the next box. Hope, apparently, never expires.

items.sourceNote: 狗的壽命比人短、每天都全力以赴的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠的一年，是你的七年。

所以牠出門散步像在過節，
吃晚餐像在拆禮物，
看到你下班開門像中樂透，
連你打個噴嚏，牠都認真關心一下。

牠不是太誇張，
是牠時間少，所以選擇把每一刻都活成大事。

而我們呢？

我們等週末、等假期、等退休，
等一個「終於有空」的未來——
好像今天只是用來撐到明天。

養狗才懂：
牠用十幾年的時間提醒你一件事：
你以為你還有很多時間，但牠正在示範什麼叫「把今天當成全部」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近總是說「等忙完再說」的朋友——他看完會立刻去抱一下家裡那個老師
variations: 進階版：你看完抬頭看牠。牠正趴在地板上，認真地嗅一片從窗外飄進來的樹葉。你想，原來「全力以赴」可以這麼小，也可以這麼大。

en.content:
One year for it is seven years for you.

So a walk outside feels like a holiday,
dinner feels like unwrapping a gift,
you coming through the door feels like winning the lottery,
and even you sneezing gets a sincere check-in.

It's not being dramatic.
It just has less time, so it chose to live every moment like it counts.

And us?

We wait for the weekend, the vacation, the retirement,
that "once things calm down" future —
as if today is just something we get through to reach tomorrow.

Only dog people understand:
over ten or so years, it quietly teaches you one thing:
you keep telling yourself you have plenty of time. Meanwhile, it is right there, showing you what it looks like to treat today as everything.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who keeps saying "once I'm less busy" — they'll go hug their tiny teacher right after reading
variations: Advanced: you look up after reading. Your dog is on the floor, intensely investigating a single leaf that blew in through the window. You realize that "giving it everything" can be that small — and that big.

items.sourceNote: 養狗就是不停開門關門的循環，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗以後，你的人生行程表變這樣：

07:12 開後門讓牠出去
07:14 開後門讓牠進來
07:16 開後門讓牠出去
07:17 開後門讓牠進來（牠忘了自己要幹嘛）
08:30 開前門帶牠去散步
08:31 牠在門口聞了三分鐘決定不出去
08:34 又開後門讓牠出去尿尿

下班回家：重來一次。

養狗才懂：
你不是寵物的家長，你是專屬門僮。
薪水：每天一個冰涼的鼻子貼在你手上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個家裡同時有「想出去」和「想進來」的狗主人——他會笑著截圖傳給另一半
variations: 進階版：你家狗終於學會自己用鼻子推紗門。你以為解放了——結果牠開始要求你「按時為牠開、按時為牠關」，紗門變成牠的搖鈴。

en.content:
After getting a dog, your daily schedule looks like this:

7:12 — open the back door, let the dog out
7:14 — open the back door, let the dog in
7:16 — open the back door, let the dog out
7:17 — open the back door, let the dog in (it forgot what it went out for)
8:30 — open the front door for a walk
8:31 — dog sniffs the threshold for three minutes, decides against it
8:34 — open the back door for actual peeing this time

After work: do it all again.

Only dog people understand:
you're not a pet parent. You're a full-time doorman.
Pay: one cold nose pressed into your palm, every day.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose dog wants to be both inside and outside simultaneously — they'll screenshot it for their partner
variations: Advanced: your dog finally learns to nose the screen door open. You think you're free — until it starts demanding you open and close it on cue, turning the door into its personal service bell.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗無條件的愛、不在意你的成就，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠不知道你薪水多少。
不知道你今天 KPI 沒達標。
不知道你那封信被已讀不回。
不知道你三十歲了還沒買房。
不知道你剛剛在電梯裡被主管唸了一頓。

牠只知道一件事：
你回來了。

所以牠搖尾巴、撲到你身上、用頭頂你的手——
好像你是世界上最了不起的人。

養狗才懂：
有一種愛，跟你有沒有出息完全沒關係。
那種愛剛好住在你家門後，每天用同一張臉等你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近被工作壓得喘不過氣的朋友——他會默默走到玄關抱一下家裡的歡迎委員
variations: 進階版：你跟牠說「我今天很失敗」。牠歪頭看你三秒，然後把濕鼻子塞到你手心——牠回答「我不知道那個字是什麼意思，但你聞起來還是你」。

en.content:
Your dog doesn't know your salary.
Doesn't know you missed this quarter's targets.
Doesn't know that email is still sitting on "read" with no reply.
Doesn't know you're thirty and still renting.
Doesn't know your boss just chewed you out in the elevator.

It knows exactly one thing:
you came home.

So it spins, jumps, headbutts your hand —
like you are the most impressive person on earth.

Only dog people understand:
there is a kind of love that has nothing to do with how successful you are.
That love lives behind your front door, and waits for you every day with the same face.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend buried under work pressure — they'll quietly walk to the door and hug the household welcome committee
variations: Advanced: you tell your dog, "I feel like a failure today." It tilts its head for three seconds, then nudges its wet nose into your palm — as if to say, I don't know what that word means, but you still smell like you.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對「沙發是不是給牠的」的內心戲，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你買沙發那天就跟牠講好：
「這是人坐的，你睡你的床。」

第一週，牠遠遠看著沙發，眼神寫著羨慕。
第二週，牠把下巴搭在沙發邊，試水溫。
第三週，牠用一隻前腳搭上來，假裝是不小心。
第四週，你下班回家，牠四腳朝天躺在中間，看到你還愣了一下，
好像在問：「你也想坐嗎？」

養狗才懂：
你不是沙發的主人，你是被允許共用的房客。
而那張你訂的床——牠用來放玩具。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個「規則跟狗講過、但狗沒有同意」的主人——他會看一眼自家那位沙發霸主
variations: 進階版：你想把牠移到自己的狗床，牠用一種非常受傷的眼神看你，好像你在沒收牠童年。最後你妥協，買了第二張沙發給自己。

en.content:
The day you bought the couch, you made it clear:
"This is for humans. You have your own bed."

Week one: your dog watches the couch from across the room, longing in its eyes.
Week two: it rests its chin on the edge, just testing.
Week three: one front paw casually slides on, like it was an accident.
Week four: you come home and find it belly-up in the middle of the couch, slightly surprised to see you, as if to ask, "oh, did you want a seat too?"

Only dog people understand:
you don't own the couch. You are a tenant your dog generously allows to share it.
As for that bed you bought it? That's now the official toy storage.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose dog signed exactly zero of the house rules — they'll glance at the reigning couch monarch
variations: Advanced: you try to move your dog to its own bed. It gives you a look so wounded you feel like you're canceling its childhood. Eventually you give in and buy a second couch — for yourself.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗在你低潮時最懂你的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你不用解釋。

你那天只是回家、把包包放下、坐在沙發邊發呆——
沒哭、沒講話、沒嘆氣。

但牠走過來，安靜地坐到你腳邊，
下巴擱在你膝蓋上，眼睛抬起來看你。

沒有人教過牠，但牠就是知道：
今天，你需要的不是建議、不是安慰、不是「你要加油」。
你需要的是「我在這裡，不會走」。

養狗才懂：
有些日子，全世界都跟你說「你要怎麼怎麼」，
只有牠不說話、只負責陪你。

那種懂，比任何句子都長。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給一個今天過得很重的朋友——他會抬頭看一眼那位無聲的同事
variations: 進階版：你後來偷偷哭了一下。牠沒有抬頭，只是更用力把下巴壓在你膝蓋上——好像在說：我知道，我會多坐一會兒。

en.content:
You don't have to explain anything.

You just walked in, put your bag down, sat on the edge of the couch and stared at the floor —
no tears, no words, not even a sigh.

But your dog walks over, quietly sits at your feet,
rests its chin on your knee, and looks up at you.

Nobody taught it this. It just knows:
today, you don't need advice, or reassurance, or "you got this."
You need someone to say "I'm here, I'm not going anywhere" — without saying anything at all.

Only dog people understand:
some days, the whole world is telling you what you should do.
The only one not talking is the one who just stays.

That kind of understanding is longer than any sentence.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's having a heavy day — they'll look over at the silent coworker on the floor
variations: Advanced: later, you let yourself cry a little. Your dog doesn't look up — it just presses its chin harder into your knee, as if to say, I know. I'll stay a little longer.

items.sourceNote: 狗毛掉滿身的養狗日常，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你以為你今天穿得很整齊。

你照鏡子三次、噴香水、檢查領口。
出門前牠靠過來，用整個身體在你大腿側面磨了三秒。

你低頭一看——
黑褲子上多了一層新的、淺色的、毛茸茸的圖層。

你嘆口氣，拿出那個已經被你用到變形的滾筒膠帶。
滾完左邊滾右邊，滾完前面滾後面。
滾到最後你才發現：
膠帶上的毛比你頭上的還多。

養狗才懂：
你出門前的最後一個動作不是擦口紅、不是綁鞋帶，
是把自己從一隻狗身上剝下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個包包裡常備滾筒的朋友——他會默默把今天滾下來的那球毛拿出來看
variations: 進階版：你後來放棄了。直接買了跟狗毛同色的衣服，從此再也沒人看得出來——除了你自己，你知道那是「整套衣服都是毛」的高級偽裝。

en.content:
You thought you dressed sharp today.

You checked the mirror three times, sprayed perfume, fixed your collar.
Then, right before leaving, your dog leaned in and rubbed its entire body against your thigh for three solid seconds.

You look down —
your black pants now have a new, lighter, fluffier topcoat.

You sigh and pull out that lint roller, the one bent out of shape from years of service.
Roll the left leg, then the right. Roll the front, then the back.
By the end, the tape has more hair on it than your own head does.

Only dog people understand:
the last thing you do before walking out the door isn't lipstick or shoelaces —
it's peeling yourself off of a dog.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who keeps a lint roller in their bag — they'll quietly inspect today's hair harvest
variations: Advanced: eventually you gave up. You started buying clothes the exact color of your dog's coat. Nobody can tell anymore — except you, who knows the whole outfit is technically fur in disguise.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗占床導致主人睡邊邊的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
床是雙人床。

第一年，牠睡腳邊。
第二年，牠睡你旁邊。
第三年，牠睡在你跟另一半中間。
第四年，你跟另一半各睡在床的兩個邊角，中間是一隻四腳張開、佔據王座的毛球。

你半夜想翻身，腿被壓住。
你想拉棉被，棉被被牠當成抱枕。
你想小聲問另一半「你還醒嗎？」，牠先抬頭，眼神寫著：「你吵到我了。」

養狗才懂：
你不是睡在自己的床上，你是住在牠的領土上。
而你跟另一半，只是兩個被允許共眠的隨從。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一對「床被狗強佔」的伴侶——他們會互相看一眼，再看一眼中間那位皇帝
variations: 進階版：你後來認真考慮買加大床。買回來的第一晚你滿懷期待，結果牠還是睡正中間——只是占的比例變得更精準了一點。

en.content:
It's a queen-size bed.

Year one, your dog slept by your feet.
Year two, it slept next to you.
Year three, it slept between you and your partner.
Year four, you and your partner each cling to opposite corners of the bed, while a fully sprawled fur-ball occupies the throne in the middle.

You try to roll over — your leg is pinned.
You try to pull the blanket — it's currently a body pillow.
You whisper to your partner, "are you still awake?" The dog lifts its head first, eyes saying, "you're disturbing me."

Only dog people understand:
you don't sleep in your bed. You live on its territory.
You and your partner are simply two attendants allowed to share the royal chambers.
en.editorNote: Send to any couple whose bed has been annexed — they'll glance at each other, then at the emperor in the middle
variations: Advanced: you eventually upgrade to a king. The first night, you go in full of hope. The dog still sleeps in the dead center — just at a slightly more precise ratio than before.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看你出門前那種捨不得的眼神，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你早上拿起鑰匙，牠就懂了。

沒有人教過牠「上班」是什麼，
但牠看到你穿外套、拿包包、走向門口的那一刻，
會先把頭低下來，再從很遠的地方抬眼看你，
眼神寫著兩個字：留下。

你蹲下去摸牠的頭，跟牠說：
「我下班就回來，今天不加班。」
牠不會回答，只會把臉埋進你掌心，多停一秒。

那一秒，你會很想打電話請假。
但你沒有，你出門了。

養狗才懂：
你每天出門的力氣，有一半是用來不回頭看牠的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天早上都要心軟一次的主人——他出門前會多蹲一下
variations: 進階版：你下班開門的瞬間，牠衝過來的速度比早上你出門的猶豫還快——好像在說：「我等的就是這一刻，每一天都值得。」

en.content:
You pick up your keys in the morning, and your dog already knows.

Nobody taught it what "going to work" means,
but the moment it sees you putting on a coat, grabbing your bag, walking toward the door,
it lowers its head, then looks up at you from across the room,
eyes saying just one word: stay.

You crouch down, scratch its head, and promise:
"I'll be back after work. No overtime today."
It doesn't answer — it just buries its face into your palm for one extra second.

In that one second, you seriously consider calling in sick.
But you don't. You leave anyway.

Only dog people understand:
half the energy you spend leaving the house every day is spent not turning around.
en.editorNote: Send to an owner whose heart breaks a little every morning — they'll linger an extra second at the door
variations: Advanced: when you come home, the speed at which your dog runs to the door is faster than the hesitation you felt leaving in the morning — as if to say, "this is the moment I was waiting for. Every day, worth it."

items.sourceNote: 養狗多年後對「家」的重新定義，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你以前覺得家是一個地址。

是那個房號、那道門、那個你下班會回去的座標。

但養狗以後你慢慢發現，家不是地點。

家是你打開門的那一刻，有一隻小傢伙從沙發上滾下來、用全身的速度衝向你；
家是你在廚房煮飯，牠就在你腳邊半睡半醒地守著；
家是你窩在客廳追劇追到一半，牠默默走過來把下巴擱在你大腿上。

你後來才明白：
你買的房子、租的公寓，那些都只是空間。
是因為有牠在裡面等你，那裡才變成了家。

養狗才懂：
地址會換、家具會搬、城市可以變，
只要你還能聽到那雙小腳掌啪嗒啪嗒從遠處跑來，
你就還在家裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個搬過家、但「家」一直跟著的人——他會看一眼睡在腳邊的那位
variations: 進階版：有一天你出差，住在一間很好的飯店裡。床很大、被子很厚、窗外是夜景。但你睡不好。因為家不在那裡，家在你訊息裡那張牠等你的照片裡。

en.content:
You used to think home was an address.

A unit number, a door, the coordinates you came back to after work.

But after getting a dog, you slowly realized: home isn't a location.

Home is the moment you open the door and a small creature tumbles off the couch and charges at you with its entire body;
home is when you're cooking in the kitchen and it half-sleeps by your feet, on guard;
home is when you're three episodes deep on the couch and it quietly walks over and rests its chin on your thigh.

Eventually you understand:
the place you bought, the apartment you rent — those are just rooms.
It's the small creature waiting inside that turns the rooms into a home.

Only dog people understand:
addresses change, furniture moves, cities can be swapped,
but as long as you can still hear those little paws pattering toward you from across the floor,
you are still home.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who's moved cities but kept their "home" beside them — they'll glance at the one asleep at their feet
variations: Advanced: one day you travel for work and stay at a beautiful hotel. Big bed, thick blanket, city lights outside. But you can't sleep. Because home isn't there — home is in that photo on your phone, the one of your dog waiting by the door.
